title: ammocharis angolensis
intro: Ammocharis angolensis, also known as the toad bulb or toad lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ammocharis angolensis include its potential to treat respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the roots in water or oil, and it is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
